questions.question: A yes/no question often begins with {a1}.
In a normal question, the auxiliary comes before the {a1}.
{a1} he finish the task last night?
In Simple tenses, questions often use {a1}.
What {a1} you done with the receipt?
With modal verbs, the modal comes before the {a1}.
{a1} the committee approve the plan tomorrow?
{a1} we have to submit the form today?
{a1} should we contact if the server fails?
Why {a1} the team cancel the meeting last week?
Why {a1} he refuse such an offer?
Why {a1} the contract not been signed yet?
How long {a1} you been studying English?
Correct form: Did they {a1} the report?
With Present Perfect, the question begins with have or {a1}.
{a1} the report have been finished by noon?
{a1} the guests be informed before they arrive?
{a1} the final decision already been made?
{a1} could the policy be interpreted differently?
How often {a1} the auditors review the logs?
{a1} the witnesses present during the meeting?
{a1} the evidence been reviewed before the hearing?
By what date {a1} the team have completed the audit?
What {a1} the market react so sharply?
Correct form: Have you {a1} the update?
A subject question usually keeps {a1} word order.

saved_test.name: Question Forms (All levels V3)
saved_test.description: Standard V3 package for Question Forms across A1-C2.
